response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with a strong testing culture and some genuinely relevant low-latency systems work, but he clearly misses the core minimum qualification: roughly seven months of professional C++ at National Instruments plus a C++ thesis project falls far short of the required 8 years, and C++ is absent from his skills list. His large-scale infrastructure exposure (~2 years of high-throughput microservices in DLang) is also below the 5-year bar, and his leadership experience is informal (workshops, dojos, open-source founding) rather than the cross-team technical-lead role this position demands.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
15+ years of broad engineering experience with a strong testing and software-craftsmanship culture (TDD, test frameworks, workshops)
Demonstrated ownership and initiative, including founding and operating an open-source geo-data platform deployable on Kubernetes
concerns: C++ experience is roughly 7 months professionally, far short of the 8-year minimum for this C++-centric role
No formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers or driving cross-team alignment
Career is centered on web and product development rather than cloud compute platforms, storage, or hardware-adjacent infrastructure
